name: Fit Check / OOTD
description: Quick visual demo of a complete outfit — typically 1-3 looks in one Reel with beat-synced transitions (snap, jump-cut, mirror-flash). 7-20s, 9:16. OOTD format. Visual-first, designed for muted scrolling. Often paired with a trending audio bed (e.g. 'Dame un Grrr', '212') and a brand-tag overlay per item. Low daily-content barrier — a new outfit a day is the whole point. Reference-required when real garments are shown (AGENTS.md hard rule #3) — same gate as fashion-lookbook. Distinct from fashion-lookbook: this is fast / OOTD / daily; lookbook is editorial / 15-30s / brand-piece.

stackSummary.keyframes: google/gemini-3-pro-image-preview via OpenRouter — one full-body / mirror-cam keyframe per outfit, multi-ref WITH user-supplied garment photos when real items are shown (required, see referenceNotes)
stackSummary.i2v: kwaivgi/kling-v3.0-pro via OpenRouter, 5s clips per outfit (1-3 total). Motion: slow spin, mirror-cam pan from waist-up, hands-on-hip pose, walk-toward-camera. generate_audio: false.
stackSummary.voiceover: Typically NONE. Text overlays + brand tags carry the read. If a single-line VO is needed (rare): ElevenLabs eleven_multilingual_v2, one short call (≤ 5 words), e.g. 'Today's fit.'
stackSummary.captions: Brand-tag overlays are static text per outfit (item + price + handle), not auto-captions. If a VO line is added, MinimalCaptions only — never Hormozi here, it fights the visual.
stackSummary.music: ElevenLabs Music — one trend-bed track (or licensed track passed in by the user). Style: hyperpop / amapiano / 90s-house / drill / latin-trap depending on niche. BPM matches the cut count: 1 outfit ≈ any tempo; 3 outfits ≈ ≥ 100 BPM with strong on-beat hits at the transitions. Composition cuts every keyframe ON the beat — this is the format's defining mechanic.
stackSummary.compose: Remotion-composed beat-synced transitions: snap (hard cut on downbeat), mirror-flash (white frame flash for 2 frames), jump-cut (camera-stutter, 3 cuts in 0.4s). Hold final pose 1-2s with an end-card / handle tag.

constants.structure: 0-1s mirror entrance / hook → 1-15s outfit transitions on beat → 15-20s final hold + signature pose
constants.transitionRule: every outfit-change lands ON a beat downbeat — no off-beat cuts
constants.brandTagPosition: corner-tag (small, lower-third) by default; large-overlay (centered, animated entry) for hero items

variationAxes: outfit count (1 / 2 / 3) — 1 = single-look hero, 2 = day/night or before/after styling, 3 = thrift haul / week-of-fits
transition style (snap / mirror-flash / jump-cut) — pick one and stay consistent within a video
niche (streetwear, formal, second-hand / thrift, modesty, workwear, fast-fashion-haul, Y2K, quiet-luxury)
brand-tag style (corner-tag small lower-third vs large-overlay centered with animated entry)
music vibe (hyperpop, amapiano, latin-trap, drill, 90s-house, trending-audio-of-the-week)
subject framing (mirror-selfie waist-up, full-body cropped, slow-spin in-room, walk-toward-camera)
doNotCopyLiterally: Outfit choices, prompt strings, brand tags, music track. Vibe reference only — copy the cadence, not the wardrobe.
referenceNotes: When the brief mentions real garments, brands, or items the user actually owns — at least one garment photo (flat-lay, hanger shot, or worn-in mirror selfie) must exist at workspace/projects/<id>/assets/uploaded/. Same gate as fashion-lookbook and AGENTS.md hard rule #3 — AI-improvised branding on a real garment always reads as fake (logo distortion, weave wrong, fit wrong). For pure-fictional / AI-styling demos with no named brand, the gate may be waived with a logged 'no-ref-consent' stage.
